content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 21, 1993

Copy A. King
Office of Brad Sherman
State Board of Equalization
901 Wilshire Boulevard, Suite 210
Santa Monica, CA 90401

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-275

Dear Mr. King:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
July 15, 1993 wherein we discussed your request for written advice
regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹ You requested advice on behalf of Mr. Brad Sherman,
the Chairman of the State Board of Equalization.

You submitted copies of two flyers for our review and asked
if they qualify under the exception in Regulation
18901(b)(9)(A)(2). Even though flyer "A" prominently "features"
Mr. Sherman's name in large, boldface type, I informed you that
both flyers fall under the exception for an announcement of an
official agency event.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

JM:JS:ak

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether two agency flyers submitted by the Office of Brad Sherman, State Board of Equalization Chairman, qualify under the mass mailing exception in Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A)(2) of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Both flyers qualify under the exception for an announcement of an official agency event, regardless of whether one flyer prominently features Mr. Sherman's name in large, boldface type.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; instead contains conversational advice in letter format. The legal question and FPPC's conclusion were extracted from the letter's body text. File number A-93-275 indicates this is a formal advice letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two agency flyers submitted by the Office of Brad Sherman, State Board of Equalization Chairman, qualify under the mass mailing exception in Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A)(2) of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Both flyers qualify under the exception for an announcement of an official agency event, regardless of whether one flyer prominently features Mr. Sherman's name in large, boldface type.
